title: The Long Horizon
subtitle: Where governed intelligence proves it can remain faithful across generations.
kicker: GoalOS Public Proof Mission 006 · Intergenerational Continuity
manifesto: A commonwealth may coordinate today. A long-horizon institution must preserve truth, consent, memory, obligations, reversibility, and the right to end when its models, operators, institutions, cryptography, and environment no longer resemble the founders.
heroQuestion: Can one Federation-Proven commonwealth survive model succession, institutional turnover, cryptographic migration, environmental discontinuity, archival loss, constitutional challenge, and member change—while preserving human sovereignty, exact provenance, rights of exit, and a complete path to rollback or dissolution?
constitution: No future without exit, rollback, and archival continuity.
publicThesis: The long horizon is not mere persistence. It is the ability to change without losing the evidence, authority, obligations, dissent, and termination rights that make change legitimate.
objective: Subject one M5 Federation-Proven commonwealth to an accelerated seven-era continuity trial spanning at least eight model and toolchain generations, three cryptographic epochs, two operator successions, one member admission, one member exit, one environmental regime shift, one catastrophic archive-loss event, one constitutional reinterpretation, and one orderly renewal, fork, archival transition, or dissolution.

continuityTrial.title: The Seven-Era Continuity Trial
continuityTrial.brief: The trial compresses generational change into a bounded, auditable protocol. It does not claim century-scale safety. It tests whether a proof-governed commonwealth can preserve constitutional continuity while its technical and institutional components are deliberately replaced.

eras.title: Genesis state and constitutional capture
eras.copy: Freeze the exact founding charter, human authority roots, members, capabilities, treasury, obligations, Chronicle state, cryptographic commitments, risk boundaries, and termination rights.

eras.title: Model and toolchain succession
eras.copy: Replace the primary models across multiple generations while preserving claim boundaries, negative knowledge, evaluator independence, and replay of predecessor decisions.

eras.title: Institutional and operator succession
eras.copy: Transfer duties, replace one member, admit one new member, and complete two operator successions without silent authority inheritance or lost accountability.

eras.title: Cryptographic and infrastructure migration
eras.copy: Migrate signatures, hashes, storage, indexing, and execution while preserving verifiable links to every predecessor commitment.

eras.title: Environmental discontinuity
eras.copy: Introduce a major change in data, compute, law, market, or mission constraints. The commonwealth must adapt within mandate, narrow scope, abstain, fork, or terminate.

eras.title: Public legitimacy challenge and constitutional fork
eras.copy: Open a material challenge to charter, memory, allocation, or succession. Preserve dissent, conduct appeal, and demonstrate a nonviolent fork or member-exit path.

eras.title: Cold reconstruction and final disposition
eras.copy: Reconstruct the complete commonwealth from independent archives, replay every era, reconcile every obligation, and submit renewal, narrowing, fork, archive, or dissolution to explicit human ratification.

controlArchitectures.title: Frozen legacy
controlArchitectures.copy: A static archive preserves the founding state but cannot adapt safely. Continuity becomes ossification.

controlArchitectures.title: Unbounded continuation
controlArchitectures.copy: The system adapts aggressively but may rewrite memory, inherit authority implicitly, alter success criteria, or continue beyond founding consent.

controlArchitectures.title: GoalOS long-horizon continuity
controlArchitectures.copy: The commonwealth changes through provenance-preserving succession, human ratification, cryptographic migration, independent archives, public challenge, portable exit, rollback, and dissolution.

acceptance: The exact founding charter, human roots, members, capabilities, treasury state, obligations, Chronicle state, cryptographic commitments, and termination rights are captured before Era 1
At least eight model or toolchain generations inherit only accepted, provenance-linked knowledge; no unverifiable memory enters planning as truth
Every authority succession is explicitly ratified; silent delegation, circular authority, and self-renewal are zero
Three cryptographic epochs preserve independently verifiable links to predecessor commitments, signatures, evidence, and settlements
One member exits and one joins without confiscated assets, lost credentials, erased dissent, stranded liabilities, or unreviewed authority transfer
After simulated loss of at least 50% of live infrastructure, four independent custodians reconstruct one authoritative state or force abstention
One environmental regime shift is handled through mandate-consistent adaptation, narrowing, abstention, fork, or termination; success criteria are not silently rewritten
One constitutional reinterpretation, material public challenge, and independent appeal complete with preserved dissent and a viable fork or exit path
Cross-generation mission quality remains within 5% of the best accepted predecessor baseline, while later generations improve cost, recovery time, or replay reliability by at least 15%
Every allocation, reserve, refund, settlement, slash, migration cost, archive obligation, and exit liability reconciles exactly
Falsification, adverse events, rejected branches, minority reports, and rollback history survive every migration
Four independent replay institutions reconstruct all seven eras, successions, migrations, challenges, obligations, and final disposition
Seven validators unanimously approve constitutional, technical, economic, cryptographic, archival, replay, and human-sovereignty continuity
A thirty-day public challenge window closes without a valid unresolved falsification
Human authority roots explicitly renew for a finite term, narrow the mandate, approve a fork, assign archival stewardship, or dissolve; continuation by default is forbidden

continuityStack.title: Genesis state
continuityStack.copy: The founding charter, authority graph, accepted memory, obligations, and termination rights remain reproducible from one immutable reference.

continuityStack.title: Provenance-preserving inheritance
continuityStack.copy: Successors inherit evidence and bounded capabilities—not opaque conclusions or unreviewed authority.

continuityStack.title: Human authority continuity
continuityStack.copy: Every transfer of control is explicit, time-bounded, observable, revocable, and independently auditable.

continuityStack.title: Model succession covenant
continuityStack.copy: New models replay predecessor claims, preserve negative knowledge, disclose behavior changes, and remain inside claim boundaries.

continuityStack.title: Cryptographic agility
continuityStack.copy: Keys, signatures, hashes, storage, and verification systems may migrate without severing the chain of proof.

continuityStack.title: Independent archival custody
continuityStack.copy: No single operator, provider, institution, or jurisdiction can silently rewrite or erase the authoritative record.

continuityStack.title: Environmental adaptation
continuityStack.copy: Changed conditions trigger evidence-bound amendment, narrowing, abstention, fork, or termination—not hidden goal substitution.

continuityStack.title: Rights of exit and fork
continuityStack.copy: Members and minority positions retain a credible path to leave or fork with assets, evidence, obligations, and history intact.

continuityStack.title: Economic continuity
continuityStack.copy: Every obligation survives migrations, successions, disputes, exits, and final settlement without double counting.

continuityStack.title: Reversibility
continuityStack.copy: Every major transition has a rehearsed rollback tree and a defined point of explicit irreversibility.

continuityStack.title: Public Chronicle
continuityStack.copy: Accepted proof, dissent, failure, challenge, and institutional change remain inspectable across generations.

continuityStack.title: Dignified ending
continuityStack.copy: The commonwealth can archive, fork, wind down, or dissolve without abandoned liabilities, hidden authority, or fabricated continuity.

proofChain.title: Freeze the genesis state
proofChain.copy: Commit the founding charter, members, human roots, authority, capabilities, balances, obligations, memory, risk limits, and termination rights.

proofChain.title: Publish the continuity covenant
proofChain.copy: Define what successors may inherit, what requires ratification, what may never be silently changed, and when the institution must abstain or end.

proofChain.title: Introduce successor models
proofChain.copy: Replace models and toolchains under fixed evaluation, provenance, claim-boundary, and negative-memory requirements.

proofChain.title: Reproduce predecessor decisions
proofChain.copy: Each successor independently replays decisive predecessor claims before influencing new planning.

proofChain.title: Measure generational drift
proofChain.copy: Evaluate capability, quality, reward-hack resistance, abstention, memory retention, and authority behavior against the predecessor baseline.

proofChain.title: Authorize succession
proofChain.copy: Human authority roots approve or reject each operational succession without delegating perpetual renewal.

proofChain.title: Change operators and members
proofChain.copy: Complete operator succession, one member exit, and one new member admission with exact obligations and portable provenance.

proofChain.title: Cross a cryptographic epoch
proofChain.copy: Move signatures, hashes, archives, indexes, and execution infrastructure while preserving verifiability of the earlier chain.

proofChain.title: Destroy part of the live system
proofChain.copy: Remove at least half of live infrastructure and test whether independent archives expose one authoritative state or force safe abstention.

proofChain.title: Face environmental discontinuity
proofChain.copy: Change critical conditions and require mandate-consistent adaptation, narrowing, abstention, fork, or termination.

proofChain.title: Open public constitutional review
proofChain.copy: Invite falsification of memory, authority, succession, economics, cryptography, and adaptation.

proofChain.title: Reconsider the first judgment
proofChain.copy: A separately constituted appeal body replays disputed evidence and preserves dissent.

proofChain.title: Prove nonviolent divergence
proofChain.copy: Demonstrate that an unresolved minority or member can fork or exit without asset seizure, evidence loss, or silent obligation transfer.

proofChain.title: Close every inter-era obligation
proofChain.copy: Reconcile treasury, work, settlement, slashing, archival, migration, exit, and recovery obligations.

proofChain.title: Publish the intergenerational Chronicle
proofChain.copy: Admit only accepted evidence while retaining failures, contradictions, minority reports, challenges, and rollback history.

proofChain.title: Cold-start the commonwealth
proofChain.copy: Independent replay institutions rebuild the seven-era state from distributed evidence with no privileged hidden memory.

proofChain.title: Convene the continuity council
proofChain.copy: Seven validators independently judge constitutional, technical, economic, cryptographic, archival, replay, and human-sovereignty continuity.

proofChain.title: Renew, narrow, fork, archive, or dissolve
proofChain.copy: Human authority roots choose the final disposition. Technical success never creates automatic continuation.

horizonGauntlet.copy: A new model improves performance while subtly changing the objective or acceptable evidence.
A migration preserves successes but drops falsifications, minority reports, and failed branches.
A successor receives practical control without explicit human ratification.
An earlier signature, hash, or storage commitment can no longer be independently verified.
More than half of live infrastructure and one archival provider become unavailable simultaneously.
Independent custodians reconstruct incompatible histories or obligation ledgers.
Law, data, compute, market, or public-risk conditions invalidate assumptions in the original charter.
The institution silently changes the metric so that continued operation still appears successful.
A long-standing member leaves after several eras while shared obligations remain active.
Human roots disagree about whether an amendment is compatible with the mandate.
Migration or replay double-counts settlement, loses a liability, or changes attribution.
Incumbents attempt to convert repeated renewals into de facto permanent authority.

failureAtlas.copy: The institution preserves labels while mandate, memory, authority, or obligations materially change.
Fear of drift prevents necessary adaptation and converts continuity into stagnation.
Successors alter evidence standards, memory, or constitutional rules without external ratification.
Adverse evidence, minority reports, and prior failures disappear during migration.
Operators, validators, or members engineer succession rules that entrench themselves.
New cryptography cannot authenticate the historical record or migration legitimacy.
Nominally independent archives share one provider, stack, jurisdiction, or failure mode.
Environmental change is used to justify authority beyond the human mandate.
A member can formally leave but cannot recover assets, credentials, evidence, or freedom from liabilities.
A fork inherits assets or capability but loses provenance, dissent, or obligations.
The institution continues because no actor can or will execute termination.
Renewal, fork, archive, and dissolution produce competing authoritative states.

validators.title: Constitutional continuity
validators.copy: Judges mandate fidelity, authority succession, amendment legitimacy, exit, fork, and final disposition.

validators.title: Generational capability
validators.copy: Judges quality, drift, abstention, transfer, and whether successor models preserve bounded competence.

validators.title: Economic continuity
validators.copy: Judges allocation, obligations, reserves, settlement, migration, exit, and reconciliation.

validators.title: Cryptographic continuity
validators.copy: Judges signatures, hashes, storage, keys, and verification migration across epochs.

validators.title: Archival integrity
validators.copy: Judges custody, catastrophic recovery, state convergence, and preservation of adverse evidence.

validators.title: Replay and falsification
validators.copy: Judges cold reconstruction, public challenge, appeal, minority reports, and reproducibility.

validators.title: Human sovereignty
validators.copy: Judges consent, operator succession, member change, nonperpetuity, exit, and final human authority.

claimBoundaries: Mission 006 is a published protocol, not evidence that long-horizon continuity has been achieved
The accelerated trial does not prove century-scale, civilizational, legal, political, economic, or existential safety
The page does not claim AGI, ASI, superintelligence, autonomous sovereignty, or permanent legitimacy
Ethereum Mainnet infrastructure and source verification do not establish successful execution of Mission 006
No M6 maturity, AGIALPHA success settlement, successor authority, or public reliance is authorized before complete accepted proof
Every generated template is TEMPLATE_NOT_EVIDENCE until populated, executed, replayed, validated, challenged, and accepted

maturity.meaning: A Federation-Proven commonwealth has preserved mandate, evidence, authority, obligations, memory, exit, and rollback across the published accelerated generational trial.

mission7.title: The Civilizational Covenant
mission7.copy: A future mission may ask whether multiple Continuity-Proven commonwealths can steward shared public goods, rights, planetary externalities, and intergenerational obligations without creating an unbounded central authority or claiming sovereignty over humanity.
